mission.title: Install and Configure Valkey
mission.description: Valkey is a high-performance distributed key-value database optimized for caching and real-time workloads. It is suitable for applications requiring fast data access and supports a variety of data structures and plugins. Key trade-offs include the need for careful configuration and resource management to achieve optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Valkey Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Valkey Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add valkey https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3

```

> This command adds the Valkey repository for version 9.0.3.

steps.title: Install Valkey CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Valkey to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3/valkey-crds.yaml

```

> Ensure CRDs are installed before deploying the main application.

steps.title: Install Valkey using Helm
steps.description:
This step deploys Valkey into your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm install valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --create-namespace --version 9.0.3

```

> This installs Valkey in the 'valkey' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Valkey Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Valkey instance to verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: example-valkey
  namespace: valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a Valkey instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: example-valkey
  namespace: valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Valkey is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Valkey pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> Look for the status of the pods to be 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Valkey
uninstall.description:
This step removes Valkey from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in Valkey.

```bash

helm uninstall valkey --namespace valkey

kubectl delete namespace valkey

```

> This command uninstalls the Valkey release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Valkey
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade Valkey to a newer version, including backup and verification.

```bash

kubectl get valkey -n valkey -o yaml > valkey-backup.yaml

helm upgrade valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> Backup the current Valkey configuration.
> This command upgrades Valkey to version 9.0.3.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get valkey -n valkey -o yaml > valkey-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Valkey server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Valkey server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n valkey' and review logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n valkey'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Valkey instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Valkey instance may not exist or was deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the instance exists with 'kubectl get valkey -n valkey'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule Valkey pods.

**Fix:**
Check cluster resource availability with 'kubectl describe nodes' and adjust resource requests accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD validation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3/valkey-crds.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new data structures and improved performance for existing commands.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older APIs related to data structure management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 8.x, ensure to review the new data structure configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Valkey pods running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n valkey'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: example-valkey
  namespace: valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
